scene: A hopeful stick figure gazing with heart eyes at one giant sparkling blue diamond ring held up in front of him.
The blue diamond ring with a fake banner behind it reading ANCIENT TRADITION, a red question mark over the banner.
A balance scale: the diamond ring labeled 1947 on one side, a loaf of sliced bread on the other, red arrow showing the ring is younger.
A big calendar sign reading 1947, a sneaky grinning ad-man villain in a suit pointing at it.
The villain at a desk under a sign AD AGENCY, beside a stack of plain gray rocks labeled UNWANTED.
The hopeful proposer holding a red heart with a big red X crossed over the heart.
The proposer walking like a puppet on strings, the strings rising up to the grinning villain holding a sign BUY DIAMONDS.
The villain grinning and rubbing his hands together, a red arrow pointing at the nervous proposer.
The villain leaning in whispering, a speech bubble containing the word LIE in red.
A big pile of blue diamonds labeled NOT RARE, with the word RARE crossed out in red.
A lineup of gems left to right: a big red ruby, a green emerald, a blue sapphire, then one small plain diamond labeled common.
A single blue diamond on a pedestal under a spotlight, a red question mark reading RARE? beside it.
A tall building labeled DE BEERS, the villain standing proudly in front of it.
A huge vault overflowing with blue diamonds, the villain pulling the heavy vault door shut and locking it.
The packed diamond vault with its door cracked open just a thin sliver.
A tiny trickle of diamonds dripping out the vault door, a red price arrow shooting up labeled with dollar signs.
The overflowing diamond vault again, the word SHORTAGE crossed out in red.
Diamonds spilling everywhere out of the bursting vault, label TOO MANY.
A sign reading 1930s next to a steep downward red stock-market arrow, gloomy mood. Flat pale gray background.
An empty diamond shop with no customers and a tumbleweed rolling by, label THE DEPRESSION. Flat pale gray background.
A sign reading 1938, the villain shaking hands with an ad-man at a desk labeled AD AGENCY.
A frosted office door with the name N.W. AYER painted on it.
A folder stamped THE JOB with a big red question mark, the ad-man beside it sweating nervously.
A stick figure drooling and craving over a plain gray rock, a red heart in his thought bubble.
A small rock with a huge price tag of dollar signs, the buyer wincing and clutching his wallet.
The ad-man with a bright yellow lightbulb glowing above his head, grinning.
The yellow lightbulb labeled BIG IDEA, the ad-man slyly rubbing his hands beneath it.
A thick history book with the glowing lightbulb stamped on its cover.
A blue diamond with a big red X over it and the word SELL crossed out.
A glowing red heart with a SELL arrow, the ad-man pointing at the heart instead of a diamond.
A copywriter stick figure at an old typewriter, a sign reading 1947 and a small nameplate F. GERETY.
The typewriter clacking out four empty word boxes on a page, a red arrow pointing at them.
A big billboard reading A DIAMOND IS FOREVER, the proud copywriter standing beside it.
A red heart with an infinity symbol floating over it.
A red heart, an equals sign, and a blue diamond, with the label PROOF.
A blue diamond with an infinity symbol and the label FOREVER.
A plain gray rock with a red arrow transforming it into a sparkling blue diamond.
A diamond ring with a green checkmark labeled ACCEPTABLE, other gift boxes beside it crossed out.
The proposer holding the diamond ring up like a trophy, label PROOF.
A movie screen showing a giant sparkling blue diamond, a film reel beside it.
A glamorous movie-star stick figure in sunglasses wearing a huge sparkling diamond, sparkle marks.
A proposer on one knee offering a ring, an outline map of the United States behind, small repeated proposal icons scattered on it.
Pale yellow background labeled 1940. A row of ten stick-figure brides, only one of them holding up a blue diamond ring.
Pale yellow background, label 1940 - 1 IN 10. The single bride out of ten with a diamond ring is circled in red.

Split-screen two-tone background: left half pale yellow labeled 1940, 1 IN 10 with one bride of ten holding a ring; right half pale blue labeled 1990, 8 IN 10 with eight of ten brides holding rings; a big red up arrow between the halves.
The word TRADITION crossed out with a thick red X.
The villain at a workbench hammering together a wooden sign that reads TRADITION.
The freshly built TRADITION sign with fake cobwebs and an aged tag reading 1000 YEARS?, a red question mark.
The villain with a sly grin doing a chef's-kiss gesture, label MASTERSTROKE.
A diamond ring with a blank price tag reading just ??? in red.
The villain writing a number onto the blank ring price tag with a pen.
A diamond ring price tag reading 1 MONTH SALARY, the buyer handing over a stack of green cash.
The price tag flipping from 1 MONTH to 2 MONTHS, the villain sneakily nudging it up, a small red up arrow.
The buyer handing over two stacks of green cash labeled 2 MONTHS, sweating.
Two green cash stacks next to an infinity symbol, a red question mark above.
The price tag now reading 3 MONTHS, the buyer's eyes popping, a red arrow climbing the steps 1 then 2 then 3.
The faceless DE BEERS building with an arrow pointing at a small confused buyer scratching his head.
A calendar of weeks stacked with green cash, a red heart, and an equals sign between them.
The buyer glumly writing a big check covered in dollar signs.
The villain leaning in with a wait-there's-more grin, a red arrow pointing forward.
The villain holding an open playbook labeled THE PLAY, pointing at a globe.
A globe with diamond rings stamped on several different countries.
A map pin on a white circle with a red dot (Japan flag), label JAPAN.
Pale background labeled JAPAN 1960. A row of brides with no rings at all, label under 5%.
A diamond ring crossed out in red over a small Japan map, label NOT THE CULTURE.
The villain arriving in Japan dragging a suitcase spilling campaign posters and his THE PLAY playbook.

Split-screen two-tone background: left half pale gray labeled JAPAN 1960, under 5%, brides with no rings; right half pale red labeled 1980, 60%, most brides wearing diamond rings; a big red up arrow between the halves.
Pale red background, a crowd of Japanese brides now wearing sparkling diamond rings, big label 60%.
A sign reading TIMELESS TRADITION with a bright BRAND NEW sticker slapped on it, red.
A globe with the villain sticking a tag on it reading FOR SALE: TRADITION.
The villain handing over a wrapped tradition bundle with a price tag, grinning.
A big spiral twist arrow with the label TWIST.
A diamond ring with a little stinging bee on it, the buyer wincing and saying OUCH.
A balance scale: a heavy price tag of dollar signs far outweighs a tiny diamond labeled VALUE.
The proposer at a pawn counter under a sign WE BUY RINGS, holding out his diamond ring.
The pawn dealer handing back only a small stack of green cash labeled 50%, the shocked buyer remembering the big stack he paid.
A diamond with the word INVESTMENT crossed out and a red down arrow.
The same diamond with an emphatic double red X over the word INVESTMENT.
The villain shrugging with a knowing smirk, label THEY KNEW.
A vintage framed ad poster with the villain laughing beside it.
The ad poster with handwritten text beginning RETURN ON INVESTMENT?
The ad poster finishing with ...we just can't print it here, the villain giving a sly wink.
A magnifying glass held over the ad poster line, label READ AGAIN.
The villain pointing his finger straight at the viewer, label TO YOUR FACE.
A dollar-sign RETURN crossed out, a big red zero and the word NO.
The buyer holding an overpriced gray rock with a huge price tag, the villain grinning behind him.
The villain wrapping the rock inside a big red heart labeled LOVE.
The villain gluing the diamond ring permanently onto the buyer's hand, label FOREVER in red.
The typewriter typing out the word TRADITION onto a page.
A big calendar page stamped 1947 in red.
A pile of blue diamonds with the word RARE crossed out in red.
A ring price tag with a red MADE UP stamp across it, the villain's pen beside it.
The overflowing diamond vault with a red arrow pointing at the door.
A warehouse labeled WAREHOUSE being emptied of diamonds out its open door.
The proposer on one knee offering the diamond ring, a red heart behind him crossed out.
Two labels LOVE and HISTORY both crossed out in red.
The proposer drawn as a puppet on strings, the villain above holding a sign BUY A DIAMOND.
A big red YOU GOT PLAYED stamp across the frame, the villain winking and holding a bag of green cash, the stunned proposer still clutching the diamond ring.
